title: 2026 Best Supporting Actor Oscar Nominees
text: Benicio Del Toro earned his third Oscar nomination for playing Sergio St. Carlos, a martial arts instructor and underground railroad operator in Paul Thomas Anderson's darkly comic thriller. Del Toro's character assists Leonardo DiCaprio's fugitive revolutionary character. Anderson initially sent Del Toro a tiger-in-judo-kimono image before the script, which Del Toro found intriguing. The actor collaborated extensively with Anderson and DiCaprio on script revisions. Jacob Elordi received his first Oscar nomination for portraying the Creature in Guillermo del Toro's gothic reimagining. Del Toro stated his casting decision simply: Eyes. I cast the eyes. Elordi maintained an immersive approach throughout filming, remaining in character during breaks. Makeup application took 10-11 hours for full prosthetics. Delroy Lindo represents his first Oscar nomination after five decades in entertainment. Playing Delta Slim, a blues musician in Ryan Coogler's vampire-infused period drama, Lindo researched extensively through historical blues literature and documentary films before preparation began. Sean Penn portrays Col. Steven J. Lockjaw, a white supremacist military officer. Penn later won the award for this role. Stellan Skarsgård's first nomination came for playing Gustav, an estranged filmmaker attempting reconciliation with his daughter. He won the Golden Globe for this performance.
